text:
Admission Date:  1982-04-19              Discharge Date:   1976-03-14

Date of Birth:  2011-07-11             Sex:   F

Service: SURGERY

Allergies:
E-Mycin

Attending:Kenneth
Chief Complaint:
Massive Hematemesis

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
IR embolization of hepatic artery pseudoaneurysm times 2


History of Present Illness:
HPI: 67F with history of multiple abdominal surgeries, ileostomy
for Crohn's disease, short gut syndrome, cholelithiasis s/p open
cholecystostomy tube placement 1983-12-12 and recent biliary
stent
x2 placed 1995-02-22 p/w 1 day of RUQ, back pain and nausea.  She
had not experienced and fevers, no itching, no jaundice.

Past Medical History:
Past Medical History:
1. Crohn's disease, as above, status post multiple surgeries
with small short bowel syndrome.
2. Recurrent small bowel obstruction.
3. Left bundle branch.
4. History of line sepsis.
5. Nephrolithiasis.
6. Osteomyelitis of the distal right femur in 1996.
7. Osteoporosis.
8. Elevated alkaline phosphatase (this was worked up and it is
felt to be of bone origin and not liver in origin).
.
Past Surgical History:
1.  Ileocolectomy in 2000.
2.  Transverse colostomy for pelvic abscesses and small bowel
obstruction in 1970.
3.  Pan proctocolectomy with ileostomy in 1980.
4.  Ileal resection (18 cm) in 2004 2 feet proximal to stoma.
5.  Lysis of adhesions 1973 complicated by wound infection and
enterocutaneous fistula.
6.  Exploratory laparotomy and lysis of adhesions with take down
of enterocutaneous fistula with small bowel resection 8/1989.
7.  Synovectomy and debridement of right distal femur in 1996.
8.  Status post tonsillectomy and adenoidectomy.
9.  Status post exploratory laparotomy and take down of
enterovesiculo cutaneous fistula 10/2010.
10. Incision and drainage of intraabdominal abscess 11/1982.
11. Status post laparotomy and lyses of adhesions and excision
on
enterocutaneous and enterovesicular fistular 09/1973.
12. Exploratory laparotomy take down of enterocutaneous fistula
small bowel resection 06/1974.
13. s/p open cholecystostomy tube 1983-12-12
14. s/p ERCP with stent placement 1995-02-22


Social History:
The patient denies tobacco and alcohol use. She is married with
two children who are healthy. She works as a homemaker.  She
lives with her husband.


Family History:
Mother with inflammatory bowel disease. Father died of heart
problems and Samantha Kent in his early 60's.


Physical Exam:
Physical Exam:
Temp 97.7   75    142/62    20    100RA
Gen: A&O X 3, NAD
CVS RRR
Lungs: CTAB
Abdomen soft, RUQ tenderness, nondistended. Ostomy pink, dark,
bloody stool in ostomy bag, fistula with new serous output
RUQ incision healed
LE warm, no edmea

Brief Hospital Course:
Pt was admitted to the SICU under Dr. Miller.  Pt was
maintained on NPO/IVF with vanco/zosyn for abx coverage and a
plan to transfuse 3 unites of PRBC with FFP.    GI was consulted
as was ostomy/wound care.  See detailed consult notes.  Pt
underwent embolization of a presumed hepatic artery
pseudoaneurysm in Interventional Radiology on 04-06.  On 12-17 pt
had a CTA which demonstrated hemobiliar in the right hepatic
system with extension into the CBD.  Pt again underwent IR
embolization on 01-01.  Infectious Disease was also as the
patient continued to spike fevers despite antibiotic coverage.
Pt did well and was transferred out of the unit to the floor.
She was discharged home on 11-14.

Medications on Admission:
ATENOLOL - 50 mg daily, CALCITRIOL - 0.75 mcg daily,
CIPROFLOXACIN 500 mg twice a day, CORTISONE - 50 mg Tablet twice
wkly, 37.5mg in AM 5x/wk, Lomotil 2 Tablet(s) by mouth four
times a day, ENALAPRIL 20 mg twice a day, VITAMIN D2 50,000 unit
Capsule - 4 Capsule(s) by mouth daily, FOLIC ACID - 1 mg Tablet
twice a day, OMEPRAZOLE - 20 mg Capsule twice a day, ZOLOFT 50
mg daily, SULFASALAZINE 1000 mg three times a day, URSODIOL 300
mg twice a day

Discharge Medications:
1. Atenolol 50 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO DAILY (Daily).
2. Calcitriol 0.25 mcg Capsule Sig: One (1) Capsule PO DAILY
(Daily).
3. Enalapril Maleate 10 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO DAILY
(Daily).
4. Sulfasalazine 500 mg Tablet Sig: Two (2) Tablet PO TID (3
times a day).
5. Ursodiol 300 mg Capsule Sig: One (1) Capsule PO BID (2 times
a day).
6. Sertraline 50 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO DAILY (Daily).

7. Vancomycin 500 mg Recon Soln Sig: 1.5 Recon Solns Intravenous
Q 24H (Every 24 Hours).
8. Piperacillin-Tazobactam-Dextrs 4.5 gram/100 mL Piggyback Sig:
One (1)  Intravenous Q8H (every 8 hours).
9. Prednisone 5 mg Tablet Sig: 1.5 Tablets PO DAILY (Daily).
Disp:*30 Tablet(s)* Refills:*2*
10. Sodium Chloride 0.9 % 0.9 % Solution Sig: One (1) 1500cc
Injection at bedtime.
Disp:*30 * Refills:*2*


Discharge Disposition:
Home With Service

Facility:
Healthsouth Rehabilitation Hospital Of Henderson Healthcare

Discharge Diagnosis:
Massive hematemesis, status-post Interventional Radiology
embolization of hepatic artery pseudoaneurysm times two.

Discharge Condition:
Good. Tolerating regular diet and PO pain medications.


Discharge Instructions:
Please call your doctor or return to the ER for any of the
following:
* You experience new chest pain, pressure, squeezing or
tightness.
* New or worsening cough or wheezing.
* If you are vomiting and cannot keep in fluids or your
medications.
* You are getting dehydrated due to continued vomiting,
diarrhea or other reasons. Signs of dehydration include dry
mouth, rapid heartbeat or feeling dizzy or faint when standing.
* You see Hosp De La Concepcion or dark/black material when you vomit or have a
bowel movement.
* Your pain is not improving within 8-12 hours or not gone
within 24 hours. Call or return immediately if your pain is
getting worse or is changing location or moving to your chest or

back.

*Avoid lifting objects > 5lbs until your follow-up appointment
with the surgeon.

*Avoid driving or operating heavy machinery while taking pain
medications.
* You have shaking chills, or a fever greater than 101.5 (F)
degrees or 38(C) degrees.
* Any serious change in your symptoms, or any new symptoms that
concern you.
* Please resume all regular home medications and take any new
meds
as ordered.
* Continue to ambulate several times per day.


Followup Instructions:
Provider: Michael Caldwell, RN,MS,63494:502-203-4379
Date/Time:1986-11-02 10:30

Call Dr. Miller (Surgery) for follow-up appointment in 08-18
weeks.

See Dr. Miller (Infectious Disease) on 11-25 at 9:30 am in
Suite GB, St. Mary - Rogers Memorial Hospital.  Phone is 180-231-9020.

Call Dr. Miller (Gastroenterology) at 335-465-9403 for
appointment in 08-18 weeks.
